title: AC1-D-SPEC: accept durable dispatcher micro-spec
description:
## Goal and why
AC1-D-SPEC: accept durable dispatcher micro-spec: Produce and accept the micro-spec resolving dispatcher API, task/pi-session mapping, input correlation, durable task-state seam, audit actor/principal, stable errors, files, and proof matrix. Durable state is mandatory; exact T1 envelope vs narrow table remains an engineer choice.

## Scope
Implement one coherent, reviewable PR only; preserve Decision 21–24, workspace-first authority, and the plan's no-pure-mode rule.

## Explicit exclusions
No scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. Do not silently widen this bead.

## Acceptance and proof
Add focused unit tests at the public seam and integration/e2e or host-proof coverage where this task crosses a runtime boundary. Run the package typecheck, targeted tests, and the exact plan acceptance gate; attach redacted command output/evidence to the PR.

## Risks / owner blockers
Hard block for AC1-D; no dispatcher code before acceptance.
acceptance_criteria: No code test is expected. Close only with an accepted committed AC1-D-SPEC that records all eight required decisions, its proof matrix, authoritative path, approver, and date.

title: D1-004d durable admission ledger
description:
## Goal and why
D1-004d durable admission ledger: add only the immutable Postgres admission rows and first-effect hook keyed by host, binding, and execution identity. Commit before effects, use advisory fencing, and fail closed on mismatch or commit failure. This is the reusable admission seam; it does not own rollback journaling.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode. D1-004e owns append-only rollback-journal creation, prepared-state reads, and recovery fencing. D1-005c is the first concrete stable-core consumer of the admission hook.

## Explicit exclusions
No rollback-journal reads or writes, publication pointer, served acknowledgement, scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor.

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit tests prove admission rows and advisory fencing commit before the first effect and reject a mismatch/commit failure without effects. Run targeted typecheck/tests and attach redacted output.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance field: focused proof verifies only this bead's owned contract.
acceptance_criteria: Focused unit proof shows commit-before-effect and fail-closed mismatch/commit-failure behavior; no rollback journal or stable-core publication behavior is implemented.

title: A1-dev local-development journey recut: P8 exit gate
description:
## Goal and why
A1-dev local-development journey recut: after the D1 EU-host exit, prove the compiled-bundle local developer workflow remains correct and record the resulting P8 exit evidence. The landed A1 compiler (#624) and landed P8 timing slice (#664) are context only; this bead is the bounded post-D1 recut required by the authoritative #391 graph.

## Scope
One reviewable proof-focused PR only. Exercise a compiled agent bundle from the documented local-dev workflow through the D1-compatible composition boundary, then update the P8/golden-path evidence only where the recut proves a changed contract. Preserve workspace-first authority and Decision 21: no pure mode.

## Explicit exclusions
No compiler rewrite, new runtime topology, provisioning system, scheduler, control plane, generic local-dev framework, or P2/package-extraction work. Do not turn a timing target into a release gate.

## Acceptance and proof
The exact local-dev journey is reproducible from clean inputs; its resolved composition/digest is deterministic; it neither bypasses workspace authorization nor introduces a pure-mode path. Record the command, redacted output, and timing versus the provisional P8 target in the authoritative evidence/runbook. This is the tracked A1-dev -> P8 gate; no new production feature is introduced.

## Risks / owner blockers
Post-D1 only: D1-006's three-binding EU proof and owner-approved runtime profile must be complete first. A failed local-dev proof blocks the P8 exit evidence rather than widening D1.

description:
## Goal and why
Owner decision gate for D1-006 privileged runsc execution. This is a human decision, not a code bead: after D1-006a evidence exists, the owner selects the privileged-execution model or declines the EU-production exit.

## Required decision evidence
The record must link the accepted D1-006a lifecycle/security/isolation evidence, state the chosen model and rationale, identify approver and date, and be committed in the named decision/review record. No implementation test is expected. It remains blocked until D1-006a is complete.

## Explicit exclusions
No runtime code, no policy framework, no silent owner-decision substitution, and no pure mode.
acceptance_criteria: A dated owner decision record cites completed D1-006a evidence, explicitly approves or rejects the privileged model, and names the approver. No code test applies.

title: ID1-002 minimal Hydra login and consent through existing app auth
description:
## Goal and why
ID1-002 minimal Hydra login and consent through existing app auth: Delegate login/consent and signup to existing account auth so new and existing users yield ordinary accounts.

## Scope
Implement one coherent, reviewable PR only; preserve Decision 21–24, workspace-first authority, and the plan's no-pure-mode rule.

## Explicit exclusions

## Risks / owner blockers
Blocked by Hydra service; no parallel identity store or token endpoint hook.

title: D1-005c collection preload and atomic publication
description:
## Goal and why
D1-005c collection preload and atomic publication: consume the D1-004d admission seam and D1-004e journal protocol to preload bounded resolved bundles, publish the durable atomic collection pointer, atomically swap one stable core, start ingress last, and record served acknowledgement. It attaches lazy first-effect admission and unused-add rollback through those completed seams.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode.

## Explicit exclusions
No active replacement/removal, caller-triggered activation, scheduler, registry/provisioner, control plane, or retained-session disruption.

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit and integration/host proof covers preload, pointer publication, stable-process swap, ingress-last order, served acknowledgement, lazy admission, and recovery handoff.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance field: focused proof verifies only this bead's owned contract.
acceptance_criteria: Proof demonstrates bounded preload, durable pointer publication, stable-core atomic swap, ingress-last ordering, served acknowledgement, and use of completed admission/journal seams without widening their contracts.

title: D1-004e recoverable unused-binding rollback fence
description:
## Goal and why
D1-004e recoverable unused-binding rollback fence: own the append-only rollback journal, sorted advisory-lock protocol, durable prepare record, and tuple-based recovery rules for unused additions. It defines and tests an injectable activation/served-ack protocol but does not wire a stable core or publish a production collection pointer.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode. D1-005c consumes this journal protocol to perform concrete preloaded-core swap, durable pointer publication, ingress-last start, and served acknowledgement.

## Explicit exclusions
No stable-core preload/swap implementation, production collection-pointer write, ingress wiring, reconciler daemon, delete/update of journal rows, or unrelated refactor.

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit/integration tests prove append-only prepare/recovery tuple behavior, sorted lock ordering, and fail-closed unknown combinations through the injectable protocol.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance field: focused proof verifies only this bead's owned contract.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove append-only journal and fail-closed tuple recovery for unused additions; concrete stable-core activation and served acknowledgement remain exclusively D1-005c scope.

title: ID1/BL1 owner gate: assign durable workspace-budget ownership and cap
description:
## Goal and why
ID1/BL1 owner gate: assign durable workspace-budget ownership and cap. Choose whether ID1-008 or BL1 owns the durable workspace spend cap, set the owner-approved cap, and preserve the rule that a hard cap exists before public exposure.

## Scope
Human decision gate only. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode.

## Explicit exclusions
No runtime code, billing system, scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or silent substitution of an owner decision.

## Required decision evidence
Record one committed authoritative decision/review entry that names the selected owner (ID1-008 or BL1), numeric cap and units, enforcement seam, public-exposure boundary, approver, and date. The entry must state that ID1-008's hard cap remains required before public exposure regardless of BL1 schedule.

## Risks / owner blockers
Blocks public self-service exposure, not Hydra plumbing.
acceptance_criteria: No code test applies. Close only when a committed, dated owner decision records the selected cap owner, numeric cap and units, enforcement seam, public-exposure boundary, approver, and confirms ID1-008 hard-cap coverage before public exposure.
